stakeholders.Role: Leads the implementation of elderly care reforms, regulating all public and subsidized residential care homes.

Source metadata.extraction_context: The Social Welfare Department (SWD) leads the implementation of elderly care reforms, regulating all public and subsidized residential care homes. SWD oversees licensing and compliance for 450+ facilities serving 30,000+ seniors.

stakeholders.Role: Provides acute medical support but faces capacity shortages, often transferring stable elderly patients to NGO-run homes.

Source metadata.extraction_context: The Hospital Authority (HA) provides acute medical support but faces capacity shortages, often transferring stable elderly patients to NGO-run homes.
- Labour and Welfare Bureau: Policy oversight, approves SWD budgets.

stakeholders.Stakeholder Name: Hong Kong Council of Social Service
stakeholders.Canonical Name: Hong Kong Council of Social Service
stakeholders.Role: Umbrella body coordinating 180+ member agencies, advocates for increased SWD funding and trains staff for elderly centers.

Source metadata.extraction_context: - Hong Kong Council of Social Service (HKCSS): Umbrella body coordinating 180+ member agencies. HKCSS advocates for increased SWD funding...

stakeholders.Role: Operates 25 residential homes (1,200 beds), receives SWD subsidies but supplements with private donations.

Source metadata.extraction_context: - Caritas Hong Kong: Operates 25 residential homes (1,200 beds). Receives SWD subsidies but supplements with private donations...

stakeholders.Role: Runs day care centers for 500 seniors daily, competes with other NGOs for SWD tenders.

Source metadata.extraction_context: - Baptist Oi Kwan Social Service: Runs day care centers for 500 seniors daily. Competes with other NGOs for SWD tenders; reports staffing shortages as major pain point.

stakeholders.Role: 300+ facilities (15,000 beds), fully self-funded, regulated by SWD but criticize over-regulation.

Source metadata.extraction_context: - Private Residential Care Homes (PRCHs): 300+ facilities (15,000 beds), fully self-funded. Regulated by SWD but criticize over-regulation.

stakeholders.Role: Primary consumers, 80% prefer aging-in-place over institutional care, demand better home care subsidies.

Source metadata.extraction_context: - Elderly Citizens (65+): Primary consumers, 80% prefer aging-in-place over institutional care. Demand better home care subsidies from SWD.

stakeholders.Role: Informal support network, overburdened; seek respite services from HKCSS and Baptist Oi Kwan.

Source metadata.extraction_context: - Family Caregivers: Informal support network, overburdened; seek respite services from HKCSS and Baptist Oi Kwan.

stakeholders.Role: 120+ centers run by NGOs, funded 50/50 by SWD and community donations.

Source metadata.extraction_context: - District Elderly Community Centres (DECCs): 120+ centers run by NGOs, funded 50/50 by SWD and community donations.
